summary: Bertha soundboard remaster delivers crystal‑clear audio while the 1972 show bursts with blistering jams and a standout The Other One.
review: The Bertha‑remastered soundboard capture is described as top‑notch, with immaculate guitar tone, prominent bass and a clean mix that lets every instrument shine. Musically the performance is hailed as powerful, featuring a soaring Phil solo into The Other One, an electrifying jam session, and a beloved rendition of Greatest Story Ever Told, making it one of the most celebrated recordings in the archive.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviews are five‑star, praising both the recording fidelity and the musical performance; detailed comments confirm an excellent soundboard remaster and a stellar show.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Bertha remaster is called top‑notch; bass is highlighted, Jerry's Strat tone described as immaculate, mix is clear with rhythm guitar high in the front and drums well‑balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers note a powerful, seamless flow with an extended Phil solo leading into The Other One and several minutes of blistering improvisation, creating an unforgettable second‑half momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Strat tone described as immaculate with 27 tonal qualities; guitar work praised throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Solo into The Other One highlighted as a rolling bomb with great energy.
band_member_comments.Bob: Rhythm playing called beautiful and high in the mix, adding real feel.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's keyboards “lay it down” so fine the rest of the band follows effortlessly.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy (Bill) is said to be coaxing the band on, commanding the performance.
